rule: Option text must not name the underlying diagnosis when the stem withholds it
method: Built a curated lexicon of ~120 high-yield Y4 diagnoses with word-boundary triggers. For every question at difficulty>=3 with a non-diagnosis lead-in, identified the underlying diagnosis from explanation summary, correct rationale and tags; flagged any option whose text named a diagnosis the stem did not name. Manually triaged 65 candidates to remove false positives (abbreviation collisions, lead-ins that themselves name the differential, distractor references that do not name the underlying condition, standard product/protocol/test names like 'ASCIA Anaphylaxis Action Plan' or 'chlamydia/gonorrhoea NAAT swab').

options.old_text: Treat as NSTE-ACS, administer aspirin, ticagrelor, fondaparinux and refer for angiography within 72 hours
options.new_text: Treat the changes as non-ST-elevation, give aspirin, ticagrelor and fondaparinux, and refer for angiography within 72 hours

options.old_text: Discharge home with GP follow-up within 48 hours - the 0/1 hour algorithm rules out NSTEMI in this patient given the trivial 1-hour delta
options.new_text: Discharge home with GP follow-up within 48 hours because the 0/1 hour algorithm rules her out given the trivial 1-hour delta

options.old_text: Diagnose NSTEMI now and proceed to inpatient invasive coronary angiography within 24 hours with adjunctive dual antiplatelet loading and fondaparinux
options.new_text: Admit and proceed to inpatient invasive coronary angiography within 24 hours with adjunctive dual antiplatelet loading and fondaparinux

options.old_text: Discharge home - D-dimer is below the age-adjusted threshold of 780 microg/L so PE is excluded
options.new_text: Discharge home because the D-dimer sits below the age-adjusted threshold of 780 microg/L and therefore excludes the diagnosis

options.old_text: Reassure and arrange outpatient TIA clinic review in 2 weeks with aspirin 100 mg daily today
options.new_text: Reassure and arrange outpatient specialist clinic review in 2 weeks with aspirin 100 mg daily today

options.old_text: Arrange urgent termination of pregnancy at 10 weeks as the only definitive treatment for refractory severe hyperemesis unresponsive to oral therapy
options.new_text: Arrange urgent termination of pregnancy at 10 weeks as the only definitive treatment for refractory severe vomiting unresponsive to oral therapy

options.old_text: Total abdominal hysterectomy as primary treatment given the high reported risk of choriocarcinoma associated with complete hydatidiform mole on histology
options.new_text: Total abdominal hysterectomy as primary treatment given the high reported risk of choriocarcinoma associated with this histology

options.old_text: Offer methotrexate after confirming suitability for medical ectopic management
options.new_text: Offer single-dose intramuscular methotrexate after confirming suitability for medical management

options.old_text: Arrange formal ultrasound with Doppler assessment because placental abruption must be confirmed radiologically
options.new_text: Arrange formal ultrasound with Doppler assessment because the diagnosis must be confirmed radiologically before delivery

options.old_text: Proceed toward delivery while continuing broad-spectrum antibiotics for presumed chorioamnionitis
options.new_text: Proceed toward delivery while continuing broad-spectrum antibiotics for presumed intrauterine infection

options.old_text: Induction of labour within the next 24-48 hours, as delivery is recommended for all women with pre-eclampsia at or beyond 37+0 weeks gestation
options.new_text: Induction of labour within the next 24-48 hours, as delivery is recommended for this hypertensive disorder of pregnancy at or beyond 37+0 weeks

options.old_text: Continue oral labetalol and expectant management with twice-weekly review until 39+0 weeks, as her blood pressure is now controlled and she has no severe features of pre-eclampsia
options.new_text: Continue oral labetalol and expectant management with twice-weekly review until 39+0 weeks, as her blood pressure is now controlled and she has no severe features

options.old_text: Perform immediate caesarean section, as the presence of proteinuria with a PCR of 45 mg/mmol and hypertension at 36+6 weeks indicates severe pre-eclampsia requiring urgent delivery
options.new_text: Perform immediate caesarean section, as the proteinuria with a PCR of 45 mg/mmol and hypertension at 36+6 weeks meets severe-features criteria requiring urgent delivery

options.old_text: Proceed to delivery within 24 hours, as severe pre-eclampsia with hepatic involvement mandates delivery regardless of gestational age
options.new_text: Proceed to delivery within 24 hours, as severe-features hypertensive disease with hepatic involvement mandates delivery regardless of gestational age

options.old_text: Deliver immediately by caesarean, as severe pre-eclampsia with abnormal LFTs and platelets <150 ×10⁹/L meets criteria for HELLP syndrome
options.new_text: Deliver immediately by caesarean, as severe-features hypertension with abnormal LFTs and platelets <150 ×10⁹/L meets haemolysis-elevated-liver-enzymes-low-platelets criteria

options.old_text: Incomplete Kawasaki disease; treat with IVIG 2 g/kg plus aspirin and arrange echocardiogram
options.new_text: Treat empirically with IVIG 2 g/kg plus aspirin and arrange echocardiogram

options.old_text: Admit, treat as eczema herpeticum: IV aciclovir 8-hourly, urgent ophthalmology
options.new_text: Admit, commence IV aciclovir 8-hourly and arrange urgent ophthalmology review

options.old_text: Commence IV 0.9% saline at twice maintenance and a fixed-rate insulin infusion at 0.1 units/kg/hour as per the DKA protocol
options.new_text: Commence IV 0.9% saline at twice maintenance and a fixed-rate insulin infusion at 0.1 units/kg/hour as per the inpatient ketoacidosis protocol

rewrites.underlying_diagnosis: bacterial meningitis with raised ICP features

options.old_text: Give IV dexamethasone then IV ceftriaxone at the meningitis dose, defer LP, arrange CT brain after stabilisation
options.new_text: Give IV dexamethasone then IV ceftriaxone at the CSF-penetrating dose, defer LP, arrange CT brain after stabilisation

options.old_text: Discharge home with paracetamol and ibuprofen for simple analgesia and arrange GP review in 48 hours for likely post-viral transient synovitis with planned reassessment
options.new_text: Discharge home with paracetamol and ibuprofen for simple analgesia and arrange GP review in 48 hours for a likely post-viral self-limiting hip presentation with planned reassessment

options.old_text: Order an outpatient MRI of the left hip within the next 7 days to exclude Perthes disease before any orthopaedic referral or non-weight-bearing measures
options.new_text: Order an outpatient MRI of the left hip within the next 7 days to exclude an idiopathic femoral head osteonecrosis before any orthopaedic referral or non-weight-bearing measures

options.old_text: Perform urgent lumbar puncture in ED for cell count, protein, glucose and culture and commence empirical IV ceftriaxone for suspected bacterial meningitis complicating mastoiditis
options.new_text: Perform urgent lumbar puncture in ED for cell count, protein, glucose and culture and commence empirical IV ceftriaxone for suspected intracranial extension from the ear

options.old_text: Reassess in 30 minutes after antipyretics as her immunisations make meningococcal disease unlikely
options.new_text: Reassess in 30 minutes after antipyretics as her current immunisations make invasive bacterial illness unlikely

options.old_text: Give a normal saline bolus and begin the paediatric DKA protocol
options.new_text: Give a normal saline bolus and begin the paediatric ketoacidosis protocol

options.old_text: Perform urgent lumbar puncture before antibiotics because meningitis confirmation will guide therapy duration
options.new_text: Perform urgent lumbar puncture before antibiotics because CSF confirmation will guide therapy duration

options.old_text: Commence aciclovir and transfer for MRI because encephalitis is more likely than bacterial sepsis
options.new_text: Commence aciclovir and transfer for MRI because a viral CNS cause is more likely here

rewrites.underlying_diagnosis: young infant with possible serious bacterial infection (likely UTI)

options.old_text: Discharge with oral cephalexin because urinary tract infection is the likely source of fever
options.new_text: Discharge with oral cephalexin because a urinary source is the likely cause of fever

options.old_text: Give 3% hypertonic saline for symptomatic hyponatraemia
options.new_text: Give 3% hypertonic saline for symptomatic sodium derangement

options.old_text: Reassure (simple febrile seizure: single, generalised, under 15 minutes, full recovery, no neuro deficit, age 6 months to 6 years); identify and treat the febrile focus; provide written first-aid advice and red-flag escalation criteria; no investigations or prophylaxis
options.new_text: Reassure given the simple presentation (single, generalised, under 15 minutes, full recovery, no neurological deficit, age 6 months to 6 years); identify and treat the febrile focus; provide written first-aid advice and red-flag escalation criteria; no investigations or prophylaxis

options.old_text: Refer to paediatric nephrology for renal biopsy to confirm minimal change disease before commencing any therapy
options.new_text: Refer to paediatric nephrology for renal biopsy to confirm the histological subtype before commencing any therapy

options.old_text: Notify the public health unit and await their advice before administering antibiotics, as meningococcal disease is a notifiable condition requiring public health intervention
options.new_text: Notify the public health unit and await their advice before administering antibiotics, as this presentation is a notifiable condition requiring coordinated public health intervention

options.old_text: Commence oral prednisolone daily for 4 weeks to treat the nephritis and prevent chronic kidney disease
options.new_text: Commence oral prednisolone daily for 4 weeks to treat the nephritis and prevent long-term renal impairment

options.old_text: Discharge home with advice to monitor urine dipstick weekly for 6 weeks, as most cases of HSP nephritis resolve spontaneously without treatment
options.new_text: Discharge home with advice to monitor urine dipstick weekly for 6 weeks, as most cases of this nephritis resolve spontaneously without treatment

options.old_text: Admit for intravenous methylprednisolone pulse therapy daily for 3 days, as severe HSP nephritis requires immediate high-dose steroids
options.new_text: Admit for intravenous methylprednisolone pulse therapy daily for 3 days, as severe nephritis in this setting requires immediate high-dose steroids

options.old_text: Initiate IV aciclovir 8-hourly for presumed herpes simplex encephalitis until CSF PCR returns
options.new_text: Initiate IV aciclovir 8-hourly for presumed herpes simplex CNS infection until CSF PCR returns

options.old_text: Urgent psychiatric admission, ideally to a mother-baby unit, for postpartum psychosis with antipsychotic treatment and infant safety planning
options.new_text: Urgent psychiatric admission, ideally to a mother-baby unit, for inpatient stabilisation with antipsychotic treatment and infant safety planning

options.old_text: Withhold oxycodone, correct hyponatraemia and treat the UTI
options.new_text: Stop oxycodone, correct sodium and treat the urinary infection

rewrites.underlying_diagnosis: severe alcohol withdrawal with seizures (delirium tremens)

options.old_text: Commence intravenous haloperidol 10 mg as the primary treatment for delirium tremens
options.new_text: Commence intravenous haloperidol 10 mg as the primary treatment for the agitated withdrawal state
